subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 1 — tags to vanilla DaisyUI badge variants
body:
Replaces the 34-tag brand-hex palette with 7 DaisyUI semantic badge
variants that track the active theme. The daisy theme IS the brand.

utils.js:
  Deleted:
    TAG_COLORS (34 brand hex mappings)
    TAG_TEXT_OVERRIDES (8 text-color lifted variants)
    DYNAMIC_TAG_PALETTE (10-colour fallback for unknown tags)
    hashString / getDynamicTagColor (hash-based colour picker)
    getTagColor (brand-hex accessor)
    getTagStyleObject (rgba() bg/border/color builder + Map cache)

  Added:
    TAG_SEMANTIC (34 tag names → DaisyUI badge variants, grouped into
      7 semantic categories: success, error, warning, secondary, info,
      accent, neutral)
    getTagBadgeClass(tag) — returns 'badge-{variant}' for JSX chip render
    TAG_SEMANTIC_VAR (tag → --color-<semantic> CSS variable name)
    resolveTagSemanticColor(tag) — reads the active theme's CSS var at
      runtime, returns the resolved value for Chart.js datasets (which
      can't consume CSS classes directly)

JSX consumers updated (5 files):
  - DetailPane.jsx: <span className="badge badge-sm ${getTagBadgeClass(tag)}">
  - Timeline.jsx: 3 tag-chip sites + "+N more" overflow chip
  - Health.jsx: 2 security badge sites
  - Contributors.jsx: tag chip + progress-bar colour (resolveTagSemanticColor)
  - Tags.jsx: list chips, Chart.js doughnut dataset.backgroundColor

Tags.jsx: Added state.darkMode / state.lightTheme / state.darkTheme to
the tagData useMemo deps so Chart.js doughnut colours re-resolve when
the user switches theme.

Tests (daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs):
  - Replaced "Tag color duplication stays collapsed" + "getTagClass is
    deleted" with a single "Tag brand-hex palette stays deleted" test
    that enforces the removal of TAG_COLORS/DYNAMIC_TAG_PALETTE/
    getTagColor/getTagStyleObject and the presence of the new
    getTagBadgeClass/resolveTagSemanticColor exports
  - Replaced ".tag base class stays deleted" regression guard with a
    "Tag chips use vanilla DaisyUI badge component" check that requires
    every tag consumer to use `badge badge-sm` + getTagBadgeClass()
    and rejects the deleted inline Tailwind combo

Visual regression:
  - 34 distinct tag colours → 7 semantic variants. Tags within a
    category (feature/enhancement/seed/init → success) look identical.
    Users lose fine-grained tag discrimination but gain a palette that
    tracks every DaisyUI theme. This is the intended tradeoff.

Verified: vite build clean (3.22s), 60/60 tests pass (count dropped
by 1 because the ".tag base class" test's second sweep was merged
into the new single assertion).

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla, tags
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
